desc:
The road's gentle eastward and southward curves become a humpback bridge that
spans the dike. Following the bridge, a big hedge has been cut to make room for
traffic as the road goes through. To the east is a little field with a fence
around it and oats growing there.
name: Small Bridge over a Dike

desc:
A thick stone wall stretches far both north and south, framing a metal gate set
within. The dirt-packed road transitions into stone slabs before the gate, just
far enough to cover the area the gate moves when it opens. A tower looming above
the gate is the first sight of Bree travellers from the east see.
name: Outside the East Gate of Bree

desc:
Towards the east, a tall and thick stone wall stretches far both north and south
and deeply rooted in the wall, a hinged gate is set. An equal note is shown on
both sides of the gate. Linked with the wall, a tower rises to the north. A
paper note is attached to the gate.
name: East Gate of Bree

desc:
The wooden floorboards here are slightly warped and grimy, and care must be
taken not to slip. The air is slightly damp and smells of mildew. Wooden walls
frame this room in all directions but west. The ceiling is low and warped like
the floorboards, but provides enough height for the guards to gather needed
refreshment.
name: Guard Watering Hole

desc:
A small path to the north connects to a shallow pond here. The dirt surrounding
the pond is muddy and slick, and care must be taken not to fall into the water.
A stone wall lines the park to the south and a neatly curated hill creates a
natural barrier to the west. The northern path connects this pond to a park to
the north.
name: Small Pond

desc:
A large wooden archway marks the entrance to the Westhill. The weathered
cobblestone streets are well-maintained, but starting to show their age. Houses
and cottages can be seen dotting the hillside to the north, while the larger
buildings of central Bree cover the landscape to the south.
name: Entrance to the Westhill

desc:
The cobblestones of Westhill Way wind between the northern knolls of Bree here.
A few houses have been built on the flatter portions of landscape, while other
homes are built directly into the hillside. To the south, the roof of the
Prancing Pony is just visible between the hills.
name: Westhill Way

desc:
The road narrows here as it passes between a few of the a few closely packed
houses, each slightly different than the last. The cobblestone streets are worn,
having seen the tread of many a foot over the years. The southwest side of the
street is marked by a giant pothole where some wagons have attempted to cut the
corner a few too many times.
name: Westhill Way

desc:
This portion of Westhill Way is a little worse for wear, evidenced mostly by a
large, muddy pothole that mars one side of the road. A small pool of brown water
lies in the bottom of the hole, and the grass beside the road has begun to
develop deep ruts from wagons forced to detour around the damage.
name: Westhill Way

desc:
Westhill Way turns here, leading into the heart of Westhill to the north and
back toward Bree proper to the east. A few houses cover the hillside, most of
which have carefully trimmed shrubbery lining the footpaths that lead to each
front door.
name: Westhill Way

desc:
This corner of Westhill Way marks the end of the main throughfare of the south
side of Westhill. A few larger homes have been constructed in the hills here,
with tall, multi-paned windows displaying a bit of extravagance amid the
otherwise utiliarian achitecture that marks much of Bree.
name: Westhill Way

desc:
Here, the wide road of Westhill Way joins with the more narrow North Ridge
Avenue. Large houses sit on each corner, with well-manicured, grassy lawns. Each
has a well-cut wooden fence marking the edge of each yard.
name: Junction of Westhill Way and North Ridge Avenue

desc:
Statues carved from wood stand tall in the shapes of wild horses dotting the
street. The flat, grassy road is bordered on either side by wooden planks laid
down neatly lengthwise. Wheel tracks can be seen having crushed the grass flat
going north and south.
name: Hummock Lane

desc:
The road here is composed of grayish white gravel with bits of red dirt leaking
out here and there. On either side of the road, rough wooden houses tower above,
their thatched roofs glistening with a hint of gold. A few fruit trees grow in
front of some of those houses.
name: Hummock Lane

desc:
A thick carpet of grass has broken free from its horizontal prison and begun a
desperate escape up the slightly sloped walls of the houses here. Gravel has
been kicked up here and there on the road and wooden planks have been placed
over a deep gash in the centre of the street.
name: Hummock Lane

desc:
Sludge-like mud has been splashed on some dark grey stones bordering the sides
of the lane. A trail of low bushes streaks along the centre of the street,
unscathed by the crushing, grinding wheels that have left indents in the mud.
name: Hummock Lane

desc:
Two large trees stand in the centre of the dirt road, their trunks acting as a
divide for passersby. The road itself is made up of a red, almost claylike dirt.
Looking closely there is a statue of a man standing between the two trees. He is
short and grey, and seems to be resting on a walking stick.
name: Oak Hill Road

desc:
The houses on this street all have decorative symbols burnt into their wooden
doors. Each is done with care, evidently the work of some local artisan. The
cobblestone street worn smooth, with and the East Road can just be glimpsed
between the buildings to the east.
name: Cobblestone Avenue

desc:
The tall oaks that give this street its name line the road on either side. A few
of these have faint figures carved into the bark, some with a few jagged
initials under the figures. The road itself is made from gravel packed with
earth and mud.
name: Oak Hill Road

desc:
The houses bordering the street seem to be rather low, featuring round doors and
windows. The doors are painted bright colours presenting a cosy appearance. Most
of these houses sport small patches of dirt in front of them, most likely used
for gardening.
name: Oak Hill Road

desc:
This private beauty salon is an establishment that offers a variety of cosmetic
treatments and cosmetic services for male or female of small stature such as
dwarves and hobbits. It is a place of tranquillity where ladies and gents of an
elitist circle come to get professional hair cuts and styling, manicures and
pedicures, and often cosmetics, makeup and makeovers. Several tools such as
scissors and combs or the famous heavy-duty toenail pliers are neatly organised
on a table set against a wall with a big mirror. A comfortable hairdressers'
chair sits in front of the table waiting for its next customer.
name: Curlybraid's Beauty Salon

desc:
Lush flowers and succulents populate this quaint courtyard. Small and cracked
cobblestones lead in snaking paths throughout the courtyard. Dried grass grows
among the cobblestones. Hanging vines grow along an arched entrance to the
south. Three stone benches surround an ornate fountain in the centre of the
courtyard.
name: Small Courtyard

desc:
A long, varnished bar runs from the northern wall toward the south in this dimly
lit tavern. Dark, stained wood lines the wall and the floor throughout this
room. Four small, round tables sit next to the bar with three stools around
each. Four stools sit under the long bar. Steel hooped oak barrels are stacked
behind the bar with taps installed. There is a stairway going up just behind the
opening behind the bar. There is an arched entrance to the east leading to the
garden courtyard outside.
name: The Taphouse

desc:
A comfortable-looking tavern stands at the edge of the park. It has been built
with a respect to its surroundings, embracing the peaceful and calm athmosphere
of the park. Flowers and herbs grow around the tavern while big, old trees
stretch upwards warding this serene place. A warm light glints from the inn's
round windows, welcoming everyone to drop by.
name: In Front of the Riding Maiden's Inn

desc:
This wide, cosy tavern belongs to Temperance and is rumoured to serve the best
beer in Bree! The brown- and gold-themed room is covered with broad floor planks
and filled with oaken tables which come in two heights; customized to fit the
needs of the big and little folk of Bree. The far end of the room is dominated
by a long, smooth bar behind which stands a tall shelf, filled with bottles of
all sorts and a healthy assortment of pipe weed. A few paintings, a roaring
fireplace in the corner and the yellow thatched roof above complete the Inn's
interior decoration.
name: The Riding Maiden's Inn

desc:
The distinctive rough red brick laid into the ground here spearates North Ridge
Avenue from the old cobblestones of Westhill Way to the south. A few houses,
also built from the same red brick, top the mounds of the hills lining the
street.
name: North Ridge Avenue

desc:
The street here is composed of a reddish dirt similar to clay. Wheel tracks are
carved into the road by the countless wagons and carts that have crossed this
path. The road here branches off to the east, west, and south, making this a
fairly busy intersection.
name: Westhill Way

desc:
Low wooden fences surround the houses bordering the road. Large trees tower
above the fences, curling over the street. The road here is lined with small
pebbles, apparently an attempt by the local residents to improve the look of the
ageing streets.
name: Westhill Way

desc:
Here, Bluff Street and Westhill Way come to a crossing. The ground surrounding
cobblestones streets is mostly dirt, with a few muddy spots. At the centre of
the crossroads, a tall, iron, lampost stands like a beacon welcoming all who may
come to this convergence.
name: Intersection of Bluff Street and Westhill Way

desc:
The street narrows here, almost like an alley that has been squeezed between
houses. Snaking vines climb up the walls of the houses in their relentless
pursuit of the sun. The walls of the houses, primarily made of stone, have been
cracked from the tight embrace of the vines.
name: Side Street

desc:
The alley comes to an abrupt stop here. Tall houses constructed of mud-like
bricks surround the street. The road itself used to be cobblestone but has been
worn down to the point where it is now equal parts mud and pebbles.
name: End of a Small Alley

desc:
A low stone wall, crumbling and borken, wraps around some patchy lawns in front
of the houses here. Covered in dead vines, the houses are built with shoddy laid
stonework. The sloping roofs above are weather torn and appear to be barely
holding on.
name: Bluff Street

desc:
A neat row of grey stones lays along the sides of this pebble strewn road. The
disparately sized pebbles are covered in mud and clay. A row of small, thorny,
bushes lines the street on either side, making it perilous to crowd too closely
in the road.
name: Bluff Street

desc:
A large crystal chandelier brightly illuminates the main floor of the manor.
Well furnished with a dark navy blue rug overtop the smooth red oak floor, this
home provides sanctuary to fellow high standing compatriots of Robal. Etched
into the southern wall are stories of victories and friendships formed in a
multitude of languages. Bookshelves line the whole northern walls and are
subsequently filled with tomes, scrolls and flasks. Centre of the room stands a
large imperial staircase ascending to the second floor that leads to additional
bedchambers. At the other end, one can see shrubbery and greenery beginning to
form and coalesce with red oak almost in a natural way.
name: Elysian Sanctum

desc:
Crushed pebbles form a path in the garden leading from the east, then curving to
the south where it leads to a small hut. Flowers grow along the path in vibrant
colours and various sizes. A few bushes grow on the northern side of the garden,
behind a small rock garden. The rocks and stones all glisten with a shiny
finish.
name: Mahadewi's Garden

desc:
The high vaulted ceiling here in this room is adorned with a heavy crystal
chandelier which cast shadows of light upon the dark hardwood furniture. A long
table, made of rich mahogany, rests in the middle of the room. Surrounding it
are a set of matching chairs covered in lush red silk. Maps are strewn about the
table. Upon the maps are various models ranging from trolls to dragons. Large
tapestries hang gallantly from the walls, depicting scenes of battles of long
ago. At the far end of the table, a grand marble throne sits, engraved with the
past deeds of its owner.
name: Telolet's War Room

desc:
Though nestled within the walls of Bree, this hall shows a distinctly dwarven
architecture. Perhaps the owner, who has chosen to live in this city mostly of
men, was homesick for the comforts and customs of the dwarves. A large table in
the centre of the hall is laden with remains of a great feast. On the walls hang
rows of trophies taken from over which this home's owner was victorious. There
are several cots in one corner where the residents and guests may sleep. A
humble man stands here, ready to help you with your things.
name: Sonlayr's Hall

desc:
Hardly any sunlight can be admitted into this small and dank room. All of the
windows on the western side have been draped with heavy curtains, disallowing
any passer-by from Staddle Street even a glimpse from the interior. The wooden
walls have been decorated with numerous paintings of coins, jewellery, weapons
and artefacts.
name: Cebol's Shelter

desc:
This large room acts as a common area and a pathway that leads to additional
bedchambers. Unlike the foyer, this room is accompanied by a hearthstone in the
middle of the room that is burning providing a bit of warmth and serenity to
those that rest here. The polished red oak flooring signifies the delicacy of
the architect work to make sure that these halls remain for an eternity. Across
the eastern and western walls are commissioned paintings given by Ryalnos of
adventures from those that live within these walls.
name: Landing

desc:
Torches in silver sconces are fastened in each corner of this chamber made of
stone. The stones differ in light colours, forming a matching pattern on each
wall, with a brilliant white mortar between each stone. A bed stands along the
southern wall, with a small table on one side and two shelves fastened to the
wall on the other for scrolls, books and other trinkets.
name: Mieldor's Sanctuary

desc:
This room is filled with a peaceful aura. Bright walls and the white ceiling,
like almost everything in this room have been embellished with diamonds. Floor
is covered with a smooth, sky-blue carpet, dampening the footsteps. On the right
wall there is a nice window, with a view to a cosy garden filled with flowers.
There is a round table with some chairs in the left corner, and close to it a
big bed, welcoming to take a nap after hard day.
name: Gilgamel's Sanctuary

desc:
This great room, built into the bottom of the northern slope of Bree Hill, looks
very organised and well decorated. In a corner, there is a great view of a wide
variety of weapons and trophies of past battles. On the other side of the room,
there is a beautiful fireplace, emitting a nice calm pale glow, as the wood
inside crackles and burns, soothing the room with warmth.
name: Sapo's Smial

desc:
As you enter the hall you notice a dim light reflecting on the huge pillars made
of mahogany, in between which stand tall burgundy-coloured walls. A majestic
lush carpet covers the stone cold floor. An illustrious sword-shaped crest of
Eriador adorns the wall across the hall adjacent to the oil painting portraying
Esmer of Calenardhon, heroic ancestor of Dunlendings. A curved hallway to the
south leads toward the inner bedchamber.
name: Grand Hall of Eriador

desc:
In the western part of the room a handful of scented, black candles are lit on a
nightstand. At the centre of the chamber there is a bed suitable for a king,
covered by a great cave-bear fur. It looks very soft, inviting for a nap. Across
the nightstand is a jewel-encrusted wardrobe holding clothes, armour and other
necessities for the lord of the manor.
name: Palatial Bedchamber

desc:
Dark coloured carpeting serves the purpose of hiding the numerous stains which
have resulted from many a party here in this master chamber. An unmade bed sits
in the corner, tilted higher on one side than the other. Several small notches
have been made into the side of the oaken headboard. A large closet takes up a
lot of the space on the south side of the room, spilling its contents all over
the floor.
name: Simple Bedroom

desc:
This particular room looks like a study, with a small desk and matching chair
standing in one corner, covered with hundreds of pieces of paper with varying
degrees of unintelligable scribbles. The faint odour of something burning
lingers, although no flame or smoke are visible. The frame of a bed stands on
the opposite side of the desk, relatively clear of debris, with a small feather
mattress and blanket.
name: Ashep's Study Room

desc:
The light in this grand room seems to fade in and out as if in response to those
entering and exiting it. Several large paintings depicting great battles, long
since forgotten adorn the walls. To the right there is a fireplace, above which
hangs an extremely well-crafted painting of Galador the Lord of Dol Amroth
fighting fearsome beasts. At the very end of the room is a large window. From
its ledge one can look far out to the horizon and even see part of the Old East
Road.
name: Mantra's Tiny Chamber

desc:
A large black stone, jagged and sharp, dominates the centre of the grove. All
around it, short grass spreads out as if from the stone itself. A faint dirt
path approaches the stone. The whole area is enclosed by a circle of tall dark
trees.
name: Shady Grove

desc:
Golden lettering and pictures that sparkle in the light cover the dark walls of
this circular council chamber. This must be the working place of someone very
important. Dwarven runes, written in gold, cover most of the stonework in the
room. In the centre of the room a majestic, octagonal table is placed, circled
by mahogany chairs. A particularly large and shining gem is noticed within the
centre of the table and was placed into a beautiful stand of gold.
name: Dark Council Chamber of Dwarves

desc:
The floors, ceiling, and walls of this room are made from solid wood. The floor
is a warm golden-amber colour with a bright sheen from careful oiling. A few
different types of wood make up the walls, interrupted occasionally by solid oak
beams. The ceiling seems to be mainly spruce boards, carefully fit between the
beams.
name: Wrong-looking House

desc:
The scent in this room suggests that the master of the house likes herbs - yet
no plants are visible. Instead, only a few small metal bars and gems lie on top
of a shining anvil which has obviously seen a lot of usage. Other than that the
house is empty.
name: Theilthol's House

desc:
An old gravel path worms its way around many well-kept zigzagging rows of
vegetables. Wooden sign posts are embedded at the beginning of each new row,
showing the various names of plants and herbs. At the very end of the enclosing
a patch of grass can be seen with colourful flowers blooming in random patches.
A clearly visible sign has been placed here stating: Please do not trample the
flowers.
name: An Old Garden

desc:
The walls have been strewn with countless maps depicting various scenes from all
around Arda. All of the furniture is sitting under a layer of random equipment
only an adventurer would need. Only the bed remains untouched and ready for a
weary traveller. A soft wooly carpet has been laid out on the floor, dyed into
forming a map of Bree and a large X where this address is.
name: A Cluttered House

desc:
This is a cosy and friendly-looking dwarven home. The walls are covered with
artefacts of different origin: Dwarven, Elven and even some items of Orkish
origin can be found. A low archway, beaming warm light, leads to a bigger room
due west.
name: Entrance Hall

desc:
This room is quite big compared to the others in this home. Not as richly
decorated as the entrance hall, only two notable items are here. A huge
fireplace giving warmth and light can be seen in the corner. And next to it, a
big round table covered with Numenorean carvings. In the southwwest corner are
some stairs leading up the bedroom.
name: Living Room

desc:
This room is rather large. The walls are upholstered with soft, padded leather
plates. There are no windows here and several torches have been set on the walls
to fight the prevailing darkness. Other than that, the room is empty except for
a big weapons rack in the rear of the room.
name: The Training Room

desc:
The most prominent thing in the room is a set of large shelves placed high
against the back wall. On the top shelf there appears to be a million different
pots of spices and herbs useful for seasoning a variety of different foods. On
the lower shelves there are huge pots and pans, the type that would make any
cook jealous. Near the window there is a table large enough for two.
name: The Kitchen

desc:
The bedroom is small, fitting a bed, a table and a big bookshelf. The bed is
neatly done and hasn't been used for some time it seems. A pile of books and
scrolls is on the table, and a lantern giving some light. On the walls you see
items of orcs Chazan has slain over the years: weapons, shields, scalps and a
crude orkish horn.
name: Chazan's Sleeping Quarters

desc:
The balcony is decorated with various kinds of flowers. You can see the tree
tops of Bree's park, leaves shifting and moving in the wind. On the floor of the
balcony you notice a soft, fuzzy rug and a rocking chair is set in one corner.
name: A Small Balcony

desc:
Dark-stained wood form the walls of this humble abode, rising up from a solid
stone base that was painted just as dark as the stained wood above. A silver
trim with an axe imprinted in every section was used to help blend the stone
foundation and wood better. Fastened onto one of the walls in the centre and
crossed are two black-handled axes. The bit on each axe is extremely sharp and
black, with it fading and blending to a fine silver all the way to the handle,
quite the opposite in colour pattern from the typical axe design. In the corner
stands a small and comfortable bed, with a small bookshelf along the opposite
wall, decorated with small trinkets, axe heads, and several containers made of
wood.
name: Aleallyra's Home

desc:
This dwelling is spare, with only a few modest furnishing. A wooden bed sits in
one corner, topped by a bare mattress. A small table and chair occupy another
corner, providing a place for one person to dine alone. A small window, covered
in rusty iron bars, looks out into an alley.
name: Plain House

desc:
Though nestled within the walls of Bree, this hall shows a distinctly dwarven
architecture. Perhaps the owner, who has chosen to live in this city mostly of
men, was homesick for the comforts and customs of the dwarves. A large table in
the centre of the hall is laden with remains of a great feast. On the walls hang
rows of trophies taken from those over which this home's owner was victorious.
There are several cots in one corner where the residents and guests may sleep.
name: Jelangkung's Hall

desc:
Beams of burnished iron hold up the walls of this small hall. Each beam is
etched with intricate dwarvish runes from the base to the ceiling. The
furnishings are sparse, with an iron-post bed just over four feet long filling
one corner, and a lonely wrought-iron chair sitting beside it.
name: Kuldesak's Hall

desc:
Large pillars of limestone support this hall, each covered in murals showing
great deeds of its resident. The floor is smooth and polished to a shine. A
large skylight sits at the top of the eastern wall, giving a path for the
morning sun to gleam down from above.
name: Athena's Parthenon

desc:
Two rows of blue, upholstered chairs sit in a rough semicircle around a wooden
stage. Thick, crimson curtains cover the windows, blocking out the light.
Between the windows, woven tapestries adorn the walls, each depicting a
different famous scene from antiquity. To one side of the stage, a small, black
door is set in the northern wall.
name: Small Theatre

desc:
This small bedroom is furnished with dark oak furniture. A large wardrobe takes
up almost a quarter of the room, filled with a variety of costumes. An oaken bed
sits in one corner. A wide bookcase sits against the eastern wall. The floor is
covered with a woven, multi-coloured rug.
name: Furnished Bedroom

desc:
This stone cottage is clearly of dwarf-make with its tall rafters and fine
hearth. Two windows overlooking the street to the west are separated by a sturdy
oaken door. A few furnishings fill the room, including a bed and table, each is
crafted from a similar mix of wood and stone that makes up the cottage itself.
name: Karanli's Stone Cottage

desc:
Nestled in the Hedgegrow is a sturdy oaken door with Dunnish and Elven symbols.
This entrance opens to a large garden with climbing roses covering the stone
walls and trees. This open-aired home is a place of mystery with an aura of
vibrancy and healing.
name: Ivy-Covered Entrance

desc:
This garden serves as both a place for meditation and a living quarters. Special
wooden frames have been constructed to support the vines covering the high
walls. Many yellow and white flowers can be seen within numerous petal-like
bracts. A small gazebo is nestled along the north wall.
name: Open-Aired Garden

desc:
Thick slabs of stone make up the walls of the august chamber. Each thick slab is
cut to perfection, evidencing the skill of the mason. A few axes, dwarvish in
make, hang on the wall. Some curving stone steps lead down from one corner into
the chamber below.
name: Stone-wrought Great Room

desc:
This chamber seems to have been carved out from the earth itself, yet it is
clean and spacious nonetheless. A small table sits against one wall, covered in
pipes and smelling of pipe-weed and coffee. A large four-posted bed sits in the
corner atop a stone foundation carved with runes.
name: Spacious Bedroom

desc:
This simple but tidy house contains one room and a small sleeping alcove. The
walls are made of solid stone painted in white, while the rafters and window
frames are of dark wood. In the middle of the room stands a huge oaken table,
and by the western wall there is a tall and broad cupboard. A comfortable-
looking chair and a fireplace are located behind the table. A meticulously
decorated giant oak has been carved on the fireplace's surface.
name: Tammi's Study
